lines: A page torn from the ledger and left on the bones. The neat hand has gone loose, the lines running downhill across the paper.
'I have stopped marking them. I do not always know which cart anymore, or which year. They come the same. They go quiet the same. They go to bone the same.'

'Down here it stops mattering what the diocese called a body when it was breathing. Sick, or loud, or a thief. They are all the one thing now, and the one thing is patient. I sit with them more than I sit above. It is quieter, and the quiet has stopped costing me anything.'
